title: Bad Bunny Wins Album of the Year, Becoming First Spanish-Language Album to Do So
text: Bad Bunny claimed the 2026 Grammy Award for Album of the Year with DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS, becoming the first Spanish-language album to win in this category. The achievement marked a historic moment at the 68th Grammy Awards held Sunday at Crypto.com Arena. Presenter Harry Styles announced Bad Bunny's name for the top prize. The artist appeared visibly emotional, covering his face and crying in his seat before making his way to the stage. He delivered an emotional acceptance speech primarily in Spanish, though he shared a brief English statement: I want to dedicate this award to all the people who had to leave their homeland, their country, to follow their dreams. In his Spanish-language remarks, Bad Bunny thanked God, the Recording Academy, and those who supported his career and provided strength to continue. Beyond Album of the Year, Bad Bunny won two additional awards that evening: Best Música Urbana Album and Best Global Music Performance for EoO. During his música urbana acceptance, he addressed the current political climate, calling for unity and emphasizing that the only thing that is more powerful than hate is love. The milestone comes as Bad Bunny prepares to headline the Super Bowl halftime show the following weekend.
